title: Bragg's Law as Rational Trigonometry — NaCl Cu Kα diffraction witnesses
_fixture_purpose: Certifies Bragg's law n²Q_λ=4Q_d·s as an exact rational identity. Uses NaCl cubic crystal (a=56402 units = 5.6402 Å scaled ×10000) with Cu Kα radiation (λ=15406 units = 1.5406 Å scaled ×10000). All four reflections verify the identity exactly over the integers. Combined with Miller index quadrance Q(h,k,l)=h²+k²+l², the full cubic identity is n²Q_λ·Q(h,k,l)=4a²·s. Crystal spread conditions for cubic (all 90° → spread=1) and hexagonal (γ=120° → spread=3/4) verified.
_derivation: Classical: nλ = 2d·sin(θ). Square both sides: n²λ² = 4d²·sin²(θ). Substitute Q_λ=λ², Q_d=d², s=sin²(θ): n²Q_λ = 4Q_d·s. No sin, cos, tan, arcsin, or sqrt needed. For cubic crystals: d²=a²/(h²+k²+l²), so n²Q_λ·(h²+k²+l²)=4a²·s.
_scaling_note: All lengths in units of 10⁻⁴ Angstrom (i.e., multiply Å by 10000). This makes all quadrances exact integers. a=56402, λ=15406.

witnesses.label: NaCl (1,1,1) n=1 Cu Kα
witnesses._note: Q_d=56402²/3 — BUT 56402² mod 3 ≠ 0, so use rational: Q_d=3181185604/3

crystal_witnesses.label: Hexagonal (Quartz, Graphite, etc.)
crystal_witnesses._note: α=β=90° (spread=1), γ=120° (spread=sin²(120°)=3/4). The 3/4 is Wildberger's max equilateral spread.

qa_connection.bragg_as_rt: Bragg's law n²Q_λ=4Q_d·s is the spread law applied to the lattice-radiation system. The diffraction angle θ is replaced by its spread s=sin²θ.
qa_connection.miller_as_quadrance: Miller indices (h,k,l) define a direction in the reciprocal lattice. Q(h,k,l)=h²+k²+l² is the quadrance of that direction vector — the SAME concept as G=d²+e² in QA.
qa_connection.tetrahedral_8_9: Tetrahedral bond angle 109.47° has spread = 8/9 = QA satellite fraction in mod-9. The most fundamental molecular geometry encodes QA orbit structure.
qa_connection.hexagonal_3_4: Hexagonal γ=120° has spread 3/4 = Wildberger's max equilateral spread (Example 7.4, Divine Proportions).
qa_connection.crystal_24: 7²-5²=24 (QA-4 Iota crystal geometry). The origin of mod-24 = origin of crystal structure.

sources: W.H. Bragg & W.L. Bragg, Proc. R. Soc. A 88, 428-438 (1913)
Ben Iverson, Pythagoras and the Quantum World, Vol. 4 (Crystal Universe)
NaCl lattice constant: a=5.6402 Å (ICSD, standard reference)

validation_checks.description: Fail detection: no errors, declared PASS
